metadata.title: M&A Synergy Haircut: What You Should Pay vs. What They're Asking

beats.title: Liam cold open — ClaudeComposerAsk
beats.narration_text: The haircut model landing at a 972 million max against a 1.1 billion ask — 128 million of overpayment, breaking even only if revenue synergies hit 91 percent versus a 45 percent base rate — made the verdict undeniable. I want to build this myself. Help me: (1) apply realistic realization rates separately to revenue and cost synergies; (2) net integration costs to reach the haircut-adjusted max price; (3) extend it into a bull, base, and bear scenario tree so I can see where the deal flips to value-creating.

props.command: The haircut model landing at a 972 million max against a 1.1 billion ask — 128 million of overpayment, breaking even only if revenue synergies hit 91 percent versus a 45 percent base rate — made the verdict undeniable. I want to build this myself. Help me: (1) apply realistic realization rates separately to revenue and cost synergies; (2) net integration costs to reach the haircut-adjusted max price; (3) extend it into a bull, base, and bear scenario tree so I can see where the deal flips to value-creating.
props.segment: M&A Synergy Haircut: What You Should

beats.narration_text: Nik Bear Brown. Build it with a CLI, then take it apart.

Revenue synergies are realized at 40 to 60 percent. Cost synergies arrive on schedule. Acquirer models assume 100 percent. Haircut-adjusted max price is 972 million. Deal price is 1.1 billion. Verdict: value-destroying.
beats.visual_intent: SLATE -- Revenue syn: 45% realized. Cost syn: 85% realized. Max haircut price = $972M. Deal = $1.1B.

beats.narration_text: Ask Claude to build a synergy haircut model: apply realization rates to raw synergy estimates and compute the maximum justifiable deal price.

props.command: claude "Synergy haircut model: standalone DCF=$850M, raw cost synergies=$80M (85% realization), raw revenue synergies=$120M (45% realization), integration cost=$40M (130% of estimate). Compute haircut-adjusted max price and verdict vs. deal price $1.1B."

beats.narration_text: The script applies realization rates to each synergy type, nets integration costs, and computes the haircut-adjusted maximum acquisition price.

props.code:
standalone=850
cost_raw=80; cost_rate=0.85
rev_raw=120; rev_rate=0.45
integ_raw=40; integ_rate=1.30
deal=1100
adj_cost=cost_raw*cost_rate
adj_rev=rev_raw*rev_rate
adj_integ=integ_raw*integ_rate
max_price=(standalone+adj_cost
           +adj_rev-adj_integ)
print(f"Adj cost syn: ${adj_cost:.0f}M")
print(f"Adj rev syn:  ${adj_rev:.0f}M")
print(f"Adj integ:    ${adj_integ:.0f}M")
print(f"Max price:    ${max_price:.0f}M")
print(f"Deal price:   ${deal}M")
if deal>max_price:
    print("X VALUE-DESTROYING")

beats.narration_text: The stacked bar fills in: standalone value in teal, haircut-adjusted synergies in amber, and the crimson overpayment zone at the right. The deal price line is 128 million beyond the maximum justified price.
beats.visual_intent: Manim B04_SynergyHaircut: stacked horizontal bar anatomy of deal price vs. max justified price.

beats.narration_text: Notice the raw synergies versus the haircut-adjusted synergies. The haircut alone wipes out 78 million dollars in expected value.

props.command: claude "Now compute: at what revenue synergy realization rate does the deal break even (deal price = max price)? And at what cost synergy realization rate?"

beats.narration_text: The deal breaks even on revenue synergies only if they realize at 91 percent. The historical base rate is 45 percent. That is not a risk. That is a certainty of overpayment.
beats.visual_intent: SLATE -- Breakeven revenue realization: 91%. Historical rate: 45%. This is not risk. This is certainty.

beats.narration_text: The teardown: most M&A value destruction starts here, in the gap between modeled synergies and realized synergies. The haircut is the discipline.
beats.visual_intent: SLATE -- M&A value destruction starts in the synergy gap. The haircut is the discipline.

beats.narration_text: Next: build a synergy scenario tree. Bull, base, bear realization rates. Follow for more corporate finance teardowns.
beats.visual_intent: SLATE -- next steps: synergy scenario tree with realization rate distributions.

beats.title: Your turn handoff — ClaudeComposerAsk
beats.narration_text: Take this prompt, run it on your own — pick any cancer type or clinical scenario you know about and ask how this mechanism applies there.

props.command: Explain how [M&A Synergy Haircut: What You Should Pay vs. What They're Asking] applies to a specific cancer type or clinical case you're studying. What proteins are involved, what goes wrong at the molecular level, and what is the therapeutic or diagnostic significance?
